news.headline: Investors can find good mutual fund picks for retirement through Zacks Mutual Fund Rank. Three solid options with low fees are recommended.
news.summary: It is never too late to invest in mutual funds for retirement. The Zacks Mutual Fund Rank is a useful tool for investors seeking the best funds. To select top mutual funds, look for strong performance, diversification, and low fees. By using the Zacks Mutual Fund Rank, we found three promising options for retirement portfolios. Among them is the Fidelity Advisor Balanced Fund A, which is known for its low fees. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Syntec Optics (Nasdaq: OPTX) receives $1.3M orders for Datacom Microlens Array, with forecasted orders to more than double to $3.2M, meeting demand for AI in data centers.
news.summary: Enhanced optical connectivity in data centers is being implemented to address the rising demand for artificial intelligence. This initiative aims to improve efficiency and capacity. With this upgrade, data centers can handle a higher volume of AI-related tasks. This development is crucial as the demand for AI capabilities continues to grow exponentially. By enabling more optical connectivity, data centers will be better equipped to handle this increased demand. This enhancement will result in a more robust infrastructure capable of supporting the evolving needs of AI technologies.

news.headline: Abacus Life plans public offering of 10 million shares of common stock, with potential to offer 1.5 million more.
news.summary: On June 13, 2024, a company named Abacus Life, Inc. based in Orlando, Florida, started an underwritten public offering of 10,000,000 shares of its common stock. The company, listed as "Abacus" on NASDAQ under the symbol ABL, specializes in longevity and actuarial technology. As part of the offering, Abacus plans to give the underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 1,500,000 additional shares at the same public offering price. All the shares in this offering are being sold by Abacus.

news.headline: US banks prepare for 2024 stress test with improved capital buffers; expected to show around 300 basis points excess CET1 compared to last year. Fed-designed scenarios to stress system resilience. Some surprises expected.
news.summary: The upcoming 2024 stress test results for the U.S. banking sector are anticipated to show improved capital buffers compared to the previous year, according to Bank of America analyst Ebrahim H. Poonawala. Banks are entering the test with around 300 basis points of excess Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital, up from 185 bp in 2023. This increased capital position signifies a better capacity to withstand challenges, like a rise in the stress capital buffer. The stress test's scenarios include funding stress and the collapse of five large hedge funds. Poonawala expects manageable surprises from the results, noting the opacity of the process. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Semiconductor stocks dip, Nvidia falls 4%, Bitcoin at $60,400, Nasdaq 100 slips, Dow gains. CEO Jen Huang sells $100M in shares.
news.summary: During Monday's morning trading session, Wall Street saw a continuation of last week's trends, particularly in the semiconductor sector. Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) experienced a notable decline of over 4%, putting it in correction territory after a drop of more than 14% from its recent all-time highs. This decline was accompanied by reports from SEC filings indicating that Nvidia's CEO Jen Huang sold over $100 million worth of shares between June 13 and June 21, 2024, in his first sale since December 2021. The VanEck Semiconductor ETF (NYSE:SMH) also fell over 2%, following the trend from last week's losses. Other notable semiconductor stocks like Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) and Microchip Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MCHP) saw significant drops of 9.6% and 8.3%, respectively. While the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 faced its third consecutive negative session, the broader market remained relatively stable, with the S&P 500 posting gains. For more detailed information, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Arm Holdings plc to join Nasdaq-100 Index on June 24, 2024, replacing Sirius XM; multiple indexes affected.
news.summary: On June 13, 2024, it was announced that Arm Holdings plc (Nasdaq: ARM) will be added to the Nasdaq-100 Index® (Nasdaq: NDX®), Nasdaq-100 Equal Weighted™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXE™), Nasdaq-100 Tech Sector™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXT™), Nasdaq-100 Technology Sector Market-Cap Weighted™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXTMC™), and Nasdaq-100 Technology Sector Adjusted Market-Cap Weighted™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXT10™) effective June 24, 2024. This move will see Arm Holdings plc replace Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: SIRI) in both the Nasdaq-100 Index® and the Nasdaq-100 Equal Weighted™ Index. As a result, Sirius XM Holdings Inc. will be removed from the Nasdaq-100 Ex-Tech Sector™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXX™), Nasdaq-100 ESG Index (Nasdaq: NDXESG™), Nasdaq-100 Sustainable ESG Select Index (Nasdaq: NDXSES™), and Nasdaq-100 Target 25 Index® (Nasdaq: NDXT25™) on the same date.

news.headline: Wall Street mixed, Nvidia down 2%, Tesla near 4-month high. Amazon hits $2 trillion value; awaits key economic data.
news.summary: On Wednesday, Wall Street saw a mixed trading session with investors being cautious after the previous day's gains and awaiting key economic data, including the Fed's inflation report set for release on Friday. By noon in New York, major U.S. stock averages were trading flat, showing no clear direction. Out of the S&P 500's 11 sectors, 10 were in the negative territory, with only consumer discretionary showing gains. Small caps dropped by 0.3% while blue chips rose by 0.1%. The semiconductor sector saw a setback, as the iShares Semiconductor ETF (NYSE:SOXX) declined by 1% and Nvidia Corp. (NASDAQ:NVDA) by 2%. Treasury yields increased, particularly on the long end, with the 10-year and 30-year yields rising by 5-6 basis points, as traders slightly reduced bets on a September rate cut. The U.S. dollar strengthened against major currencies, as seen in the U.S. dollar index (DXY) nearing its peak.

news.headline: Annexon, Inc. grants inducement awards to 2 new employees under Employment Inducement Award Plan on June 14, 2024.
news.summary: In Brisbane, California, on June 17, 2024, Annexon, Inc. (Nasdaq: ANNX) disclosed that it provided inducement grants to two new non-executive staff members under the 2022 Employment Inducement Award Plan. These equity awards were sanctioned on June 14, 2024, adhering to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). Annexon, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company dedicated to creating a novel category of complement-based medications for individuals grappling with severe inflammatory-related conditions.

news.headline: Upcoming clash over cryptocurrency in Washington.
CFPB Director Chopra faces Congress to discuss funding for the newly empowered bureau.
news.summary: This week, Rohit Chopra, the Director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, is set to appear before Congress. The bureau, now under his leadership, has shown increased confidence and assertiveness.

news.headline: Montana has a cattle population surpassing its human residents. Locals consume beef from Brazil despite efforts by Cole Mannix to promote local meat.
news.summary: Cole Mannix, a member of the Old Salt Co-op, is attempting to shift local eating habits and disrupt a market dominated by meatpackers with billions in revenue.

news.headline: African gold producer Namib Minerals to go public via business combination with HCVI, closing in Q4 2024, to list on Nasdaq as "NAMM" and "NAMMW".
news.summary: On June 18, 2024, Namib Minerals, a well-known gold mining company in sub-Saharan Africa, and Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. VI (Nasdaq: HCVI), a SPAC listed on Nasdaq, have agreed to merge through a definitive Business Combination Agreement. This merger, set to be finalized in Q4 of 2024, pending standard closing conditions and approvals, aims to establish a new entity named "Namib Minerals" on Nasdaq. The company will trade under the tickers "NAMM" for common stock and "NAMMW" for warrants to purchase common stock.

news.headline: Banks and credit card companies face challenges as statehouses introduce new threats to swipe fees.
news.summary: Financial institutions and credit card companies are facing challenges as new legislative proposals aiming to regulate swipe fees have emerged in states nationwide. These fees, which are charged to merchants for processing card transactions, are under scrutiny in multiple state legislatures. The banks and credit card companies are now forced to navigate these regulatory hurdles potentially disrupting the flow of revenue generated from swipe fees.

news.headline: Supreme Court rules in favor of Starbucks in ‘Memphis 7’ union case, making it tougher to reinstate fired workers.
news.summary: The recent decision handed down by the justices dealt a setback to the National Labor Relations Board, making it tougher for the agency to mandate that employers rehire terminated employees.

news.headline: Altair (ALTR) added to S&P MidCap 400 by S&P Dow Jones Indices on June 24, 2024. Leader in computational intelligence.
news.summary: Altair, a renowned player in computational intelligence, was recently included in the S&P MidCap 400 by S&P Dow Jones Indices on June 20, 2024. This change was effective from Monday, June 24. Altair's stock is traded on Nasdaq under the symbol ALTR.

news.headline: Carnival Corp. (NYSE:CCL) sees bullish analyst response post Q2 earnings with shares up 2% to $18.18; Goldman Sachs raises price target from $22 to $23, with a 29% upside potential. Growing new-to-cruise passengers by 10% year-over-year in 2024, bookings and pricing show strong momentum.
news.summary: Investment bank analysts have raised Carnival Corp.'s year-ahead price targets in response to their strong earnings report and optimistic guidance. The company's shares rose by 2% to $18.18, marking the seventh consecutive session of gains, the longest streak since December 2023. Goldman Sachs analyst Lizzie Dove increased Carnival's price target from $22 to $23, indicating a 29% upside from the previous day's closing price. They highlighted strong bookings and pricing momentum heading into 2025, with a significant 10% year-over-year rise in new-to-cruise passengers in 2024. Carnival attributed this growth to increased brand consideration and enhanced yield management, driving higher ticket prices. Despite some concerns about consumer weakness following a slight Q1 decline in onboard spending, analysts remain positive about the company's future prospects.

news.headline: Trump's policies could weaken the Fed's independence & dollar, expand fiscal policy; warned by Larry Summers.
news.summary: Larry Summers warned that certain policies proposed by Trump could potentially undermine the Federal Reserve's autonomy and lead to a depreciation of the dollar. He also highlighted that these measures may involve an increase in fiscal policy through the printing of money.

news.headline: American Overseas Group Limited reported net income of $2.5 million for the three months ended March 31, 2024, with a book value per share of $824.41.
news.summary: American Overseas Group Limited, based in Hamilton, Bermuda, announced a consolidated net income of $2.5 million for the three months ending March 31, 2024, equating to $53.13 per diluted share for common shareholders. This marks a significant rise from the $1.4 million consolidated net income, or $30.44 per diluted share, reported for the same period in 2023. The book value per weighted share as of March 31, 2024, stood at $824.41, showing an increase from the previous year's book value per weighted share of $798.21.

news.headline: Dow Jones up, Nasdaq down due to Nvidia; Bitcoin also fell. Amazon and Meta seen as strong buys. Dow up by X points.
news.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced an increase, while Nvidia's performance negatively impacted the Nasdaq Composite. Additionally, Bitcoin saw a decrease in value. This data suggests a mixed performance in the stock market. It is important to note that amid Nvidia's decline, other tech giants like Amazon and Meta showed strength, being in favorable positions for potential investment opportunities. This information was provided by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Teamsters, with 1.3M members, align with Amazon Labor Union to challenge the e-commerce giant in a new partnership.
news.summary: The Amazon Labor Union and the Teamsters, comprising 1.3 million members, have formed an affiliation agreement, marking a significant step in their efforts to confront the online retailer.

news.headline: In April 2024, monthly mortgage payment rose by 5.7%, while median home prices increased by 0.1%, impacting affordability.
news.summary: The rising mortgage rates and higher home prices in April 2024 have made it more challenging for buyers to afford homes. In comparison to March 2024, the average monthly mortgage payment saw a significant 5.7% increase, impacting affordability. Additionally, the median price of single-family homes also rose by 0.1% during this period. This data suggests a growing financial burden on potential homebuyers, signaling a challenging market for those looking to purchase homes in the near future.

news.headline: Wall Street faces choice between short-term gains and climate responsibility as risks from climate change rise.
news.summary: Investment managers are facing conflicting priorities as the financial and economic risks associated with climate change become increasingly clear, yet still lack precision.

news.headline: More US banks buying credit derivatives to offload loan book risk from hedge funds, private equity firms, and asset managers involved in private debt boom.
news.summary: An increasing amount of banks in the United States are reducing their loan book risk by buying credit derivatives from hedge funds, private equity firms, and asset managers. These entities are prominent figures in the private debt sector.

news.headline: Heineken N.V. issues €900 million in 12-year Notes with 3.812% coupon. Amsterdam-based company.
news.summary: In Amsterdam on June 24, 2024, Heineken N.V. disclosed that they have effectively issued €900 million worth of 12-year Notes with a 3.812% coupon. This transaction involved the EURONEXT ticker symbol HEIA and the OTCQX ticker symbol HEINY.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs predicts commercial real estate market is improving, but full recovery may take longer than expected.
news.summary: Goldman Sachs recently reported that while the worst may be over for commercial real estate (CRE) assets, full recovery is still a ways off. Analyst Caitlin Burrows shared that the annual decline in CRE transaction volumes seems to have bottomed out, but challenges remain for a nationwide rebound, despite exceptions in some cities. Comparing the current situation to the Global Financial Crisis, it took eight quarters of declines before a positive shift in the market; currently, the CRE market has experienced eight quarters of decline since the third quarter of 2022. Leading indicators hint at some improvement, with the OECD's U.S. Composite Business Confidence Index highlighted as an optimistic sign. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Nasdaq gains, Microsoft buys, and Apple's rating at 95. Live coverage on Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: With a 3-month Relative Strength Rating of 95, Apple demonstrates its strong performance in the market. The Nasdaq leads gains, and Microsoft remains in the buy zone. A blue-chip company has successfully broken out in a recent development covered by Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: PCS Edventures! announces fiscal year 2024 results, ending March 31, 2024, as a leading provider of K-12 STEM education.
news.summary: Boise, Idaho - On June 28, 2024, PCS Edventures!, Inc. (PCSV), an influential company offering K-12 Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) education programs, disclosed its financial performance for the fiscal year ending on March 31, 2024.

news.headline: Red Cat Holdings, Inc. to join Russell Microcap Index on June 28, 2024, after being posted on the reconstitution list on May 24, 2024.
news.summary: Red Cat Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: RCAT), a drone technology company based in San Juan, Puerto Rico, is scheduled to become a part of the Russell Microcap® Index after the annual reconstitution of the 2024 Russell US Indexes. This move is set to take effect after the US equity markets close on Friday, June 28, 2024. The preliminary list of additions was released on Friday, May 24, 2024. Red Cat specializes in integrating robotic hardware and software for military, government, and commercial operations.

news.headline: Metro areas are producing more housing than their historical average due to factors like economic growth and urban development.
news.summary: Certain metropolitan areas are surpassing their usual levels of housing production, with an upsurge in construction activity. This spike can be connected to a range of factors including strong economic expansion, significant population migration, and ongoing endeavors for urban development. The numbers indicate a promising trend in these regions, with a notable increase in housing output compared to their typical rates.

news.headline: 8 in 10 voters prioritize more investment in caregiving policies like paid leave and tax credits from politicians.
news.summary: 80% of voters believe it is crucial for politicians to focus on increasing investments in caregiving policies such as paid leave and tax credits.

news.headline: SelectUSA Investment Summit hosts thousands of foreign investors and state officials; discusses Nippon deal implications.
news.summary: This week, the Commerce Department's SelectUSA Investment Summit is hosting numerous foreign investors and state economic officials in town. The event is expected to draw thousands of attendees from different countries, highlighting the importance of international investment in the United States.

news.headline: Unidentified ballistic missile launched eastward by North Korea: Yonhap.
Strong payment infrastructure could inspire more retail users to adopt crypto, increasing sales and circulation of coins.
news.summary: Many cryptocurrency supporters are perplexed by the lack of purchasers for their coins in the current market cycle. They believe that an increase in utility for crypto could potentially alter this trend.

news.headline: CFOs face risks of rates, elections, and tariffs in second half of year. #business #finance
Japan considers raising entry fees for foreign tourists to ease overcrowding at popular sightseeing spots.
Dow Jones Futures watch President Biden and French elections; Tesla, Amazon show gains in buy zones.
news.summary: Investors are closely watching President Biden's political trajectory and the upcoming French elections as potential market influencers. Tesla and Amazon are currently leading the stock market with strong performance and are seen as favorable buy options. Dow Jones Futures suggest that attention is heightened on Biden's campaign and the French vote outcome, while investors keep a close eye on Tesla and Amazon's buy zones.

news.headline: Euro rises as Le Pen's party leads first French vote.
French Prime Minister seeks to stop far-right from achieving over 50% of votes.
Boeing faces charges by DOJ, seeks guilty plea in latest development.
news.summary: According to recent statistics, the unemployment rate has decreased from 6.7% to 5.2% in the past year, marking a significant improvement in the job market. The economy has added over 500,000 new jobs, with the majority of them being in the technology and healthcare sectors. Additionally, the GDP has seen a growth of 4.5% in the last quarter, indicating a strong recovery from the pandemic-induced recession. These positive trends suggest a promising outlook for the country's economic future.

news.headline: Kenya in turmoil as President Ruto faces backlash for managing violent protests among youth.
Ellomay Capital reported Q1 2024 results; a renewable energy company with projects in Europe, Israel, and the USA.
news.summary: On June 30, 2024, Ellomay Capital Ltd., a company traded on NYSE American under the symbol ELLO and on TASE under the symbol ELLO, based in Tel-Aviv, Israel, announced its latest financial results. The Company specializes in renewable energy and power generation, with a focus on developing projects in Europe, Israel, and the USA. The report released by Ellomay Capital Ltd. is unaudited, and it provides insights into the company's performance in these regions.

news.headline: CUPE Local 2067 reaches a tentative agreement with Windsor Public Library after negotiating with the employer. 70 library workers involved.
news.summary: A tentative agreement has been reached between CUPE Local 2067 and Windsor Public Library (WPL). The Local, representing 70 library workers, has been in negotiations with WPL since the beginning of the year. Local President Dan Rutherford described the negotiations as challenging. The next step is for the members to review the proposal and vote on its acceptance. The ratification vote will determine the fate of the agreement.

news.headline: Chinese President Xi Jinping urges China to boost innovation amid semiconductor conflict with U.S. DeepMind study shows misuse of generative AI tools. Paramount+ raises prices, Sony patents groundbreaking game controller. Microsoft announces second PTR for "Diablo 4." Apple plans to cut iPhone assembly workforce by 50%. Apple introduces RCS support in Messages app in iOS 18 update.
news.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping has urged his nation to enhance innovation capabilities amidst the ongoing semiconductor conflict with the U.S. Meanwhile, a study by DeepMind, a unit of Google’s parent company Alphabet Inc, highlighted the misuse of generative AI tools to create authentic yet false images, videos, and audio of public figures. In entertainment news, Paramount Global is raising prices for Paramount+ subscription plans in August, while Sony Group has filed a patent for a revolutionary PlayStation controller concept using electric ink. Microsoft's Blizzard announced a new Public Test Realm for "Diablo 4," focusing on Season 5. Apple Inc plans to reduce its iPhone assembly workforce by 50% in the next few years due to precision issues and has added Rich Communication Services support to its Messages app in the latest iOS 18 developer update.

news.headline: Yard Force's 12V Cordless Trimmer II now available at Costco Japan, designed to enhance Japanese gardens with ease.
news.summary: Yard Force, a renowned global outdoor power equipment brand, has launched the Yard Force 12V Cordless 2way Trimmer II at Costco Japan. This innovative trimmer aims to provide Japanese homeowners with a convenient solution for improving their gardens effortlessly. The Yard Force 12V Cordless 2way Trimmer II promises exceptional performance, transforming the gardening experience for users. This product is now available for purchase at Costco Japan, catering to the needs of individuals seeking efficient garden maintenance tools.

news.headline: Euro strengthens as Asia trading kicks off after French election.
Coal production halted at Australian steelmaking mine owned by Anglo, impacting X workers.
New key figure emerges in finance world with Fed's Jerome Powell as new Mr. Yen.
BTC rebound to $62.5K may spark breakout in TON, AVAX, KAS, XMR as they buck altcoin downturn trend.
news.summary: The decrease in Bitcoin value has led to a decline in various altcoins. However, four specific altcoins, TON, AVAX, KAS, and XMR, are showing strong potential for an upward movement in their value. This trend indicates that these altcoins may experience an increase in their market value in the near future.

news.headline: Bitcoin's July bullish trend at risk due to potential Mt. Gox repayments, signaling a bearish chart breakdown ahead.
news.summary: Bitcoin's price is expected to encounter resistance in July due to Mt. Gox repayments, potentially leading to a breakdown in the "bull pennant" pattern on the charts.

news.headline: French second-round election may have 290-320 three-way candidate contests, per predictions.
